briefing.headline: Rig up a Medium Diving Crankbait — finesse fall approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Medium Diving Crankbait is a strong choice for fall with stained water and post frontal conditions.

Wheeler: The DT-6 is the most universal crankbait; it covers the 3-6 foot sweet spot and works from mid-40s water temps through pre-spawn, post-spawn, summer, and fall on rivers, highland and lowland impoundments, and smallmouth lakes.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: The DT-6 is the most universal crankbait; it covers the 3-6 foot sweet spot and works from mid-40s water temps through pre-spawn, post-spawn, summer, and fall on rivers, highland and lowland
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on flat, grass, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: KVD: Position boat outside the grass line, fire at a 45° quartering angle into shallower grass. Crank down into the grass, slow up — let the bait float free or rip it free. That stop-start-rip action
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on flat, grass, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: '2008 Classic at Hartwell — brutal cold Day 1, hammered them cranking a weighted-to-suspend Series 5 over 15-25ft with stop-and-start retrieve. Day 2, post-frontal sunny skies killed that bite. Ran to the upper end with stained water and fish...
Wheeler: In fall, fish school up in ditches, on little flats in 2-4 feet, and especially around grass; on grassless lakes, key on shallow ditches with stumps that let bass ambush shad.

Palaniuk: 'For southern regions the key player is the shad — I lean towards colors with more flash, silver or white hues with a touch of color. For northern regions, fish follow perch on outside weedlines and transitions to deep water, so I go mor...

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal fall fish pull tighter to cover and deeper structure at 8-18ft. The shad migration pauses but doesn't stop. Focus on the deepest fish in a given creek arm — channel bends and main creek points.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If fish are short-striking, add a long pause after deflections to let the lure suspend
